evidence.reason: The implemented capability chain now reaches explicit default-off manifest and standalone CLI candidate integration. A repository-wide read-only audit over 43 artifacts found 33 targets with authoritative depth-two neighborhoods and 10 lineage-isolated targets. Neighborhood sizes remained bounded at one to six candidates, but median whole-artifact cost was 2,223 tokens and the maximum was 13,208 tokens. Budgets of 500 or 1,500 tokens frequently selected no neighbor or only one neighbor, showing that representation granularity and budget starvation—not edge authority or graph fan-out—are the next unresolved issues.

name: Graph-local scoped memory context selection
notes: PLAN-20260709-0002 and CAP-0005 implemented the original read-only graph-neighborhood advisor slice.
CAP-0006 through CAP-0009 added evidence evaluation, readiness decisions, and explicit default-off context-pack candidate integration.
PLAN-20260716-0001 and CAP-0011 completed standalone context-pack CLI exposure while preserving default and provider behavior.
The 2026-07-16 repository audit found authoritative and bounded neighborhoods, but substantial whole-artifact budget starvation and ten lineage-isolated targets.
GAP-0002 remains open because CLI reachability and whole-artifact candidate inclusion do not yet provide compact distance-aware graph-local scoped memory.
recommended_first_slice: Propose a separate evidence-only evaluation of distance-aware graph-neighborhood representation. Evaluate rich distance-one representation, compact distance-two representation, deterministic token accounting, isolated-target diagnostics, and local/global context balance using existing repository artifacts. Keep all behavior default-off and do not modify provider prompts, automatic target selection, automatic context inclusion, persisted graph state, retrieval, embeddings, reranking, production indexes, memory stores, or runtime manifests without a later separate admitted implementation plan.
risk: Without graph-local scoped memory, context assembly may remain too coarse: it can miss relevant neighboring artifacts, include stale latest artifacts, or fail to distinguish local task context from global ontology/invariant context.
summary: AI-Lab now provides authoritative read-only graph-neighborhood diagnostics, evidence and readiness evaluation, explicit default-off context-pack candidate integration, and standalone CLI exposure. GAP-0002 remains open because graph-local scoped memory still lacks compact distance-aware representations, target-selection policy, isolated-target fallback semantics, typed scoped-summary composition, local/global memory balance, and separately governed provider-facing or automatic inclusion.
